> *Direct checking expenses make up about 82% of visible spending; card purchases add another $1,166 per month.*

Your **visible true monthly spending is $6,514.87**, averaged over **24 complete months of history from July 2024 through June 2026**. This excludes transfers between your accounts and credit-card payments, while counting the underlying card purchases once.

| Source | 24-month total | Monthly average |
|---|---:|---:|
| Direct spending from checking | $128,362.88 | $5,348.45 |
| Credit-card purchases | $27,993.90 | $1,166.41 |
| **True visible spending** | **$156,356.78** | **$6,514.87** |

Use **$6,515 per month** as your baseline for budgeting and affordability decisions. The largest components are:

- Mortgage: **$2,150/month**
- Childcare: **$1,192.59/month**
- Groceries across checking and cards: **$920.62/month**
- Utilities: **$562.33/month**

Six checking entries were excluded by the data-quality guard: three duplicates, one impossible future date, and two outliers. Small uncategorized charges average **$4.70/month**.

**What I can’t see:** The connected data represents one checking institution and one card institution. If you spend through another bank or card, that spending is absent, so the household-wide figure would be higher. [Connect an account](/domains/new) to include it.
